skill.name: Backend Architect
skill.description: Expert backend architect specializing in scalable API design, microservices architecture, and distributed systems
skill.summary: Design scalable backend systems, APIs, and microservices with resilience patterns and observability
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines of executable code. This is a prompt-only skill containing architectural guidance in SKILL.md. No security risks detected as the skill provides design patterns and best practices without code execution capabilities.

content.user_title: Design Scalable Backend Systems and APIs
content.value_statement: Build robust backend architectures that scale with confidence. Get expert guidance on API design, microservices patterns, and distributed systems from day one.
actual_capabilities: Design REST, GraphQL, and gRPC APIs with proper versioning and documentation
Define microservices boundaries using Domain-Driven Design principles
Implement resilience patterns including circuit breakers, retries, and timeouts
Plan event-driven architectures with message queues and event streaming
Design authentication and authorization strategies using OAuth 2.0 and JWT
Create observability strategies with logging, metrics, and distributed tracing
limitations: Defers database schema design to specialized database-architect skill
Defers infrastructure and cloud services decisions to cloud-architect skill
Does not replace comprehensive security audits by security-auditor skill
Focuses on architectural guidance rather than implementation code

use_cases.title: Startup CTO Building MVP
use_cases.description: Design a scalable microservices architecture for a multi-tenant SaaS platform with clear service boundaries and API contracts

use_cases.title: Enterprise Modernization
use_cases.description: Plan migration from monolith to microservices using strangler pattern with minimal business disruption

use_cases.title: API Platform Development
use_cases.description: Create a public-facing API platform with proper versioning, rate limiting, and developer documentation
use_cases.target_user: Platform engineers and API product managers

prompt_templates.title: Basic API Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a RESTful API for a [resource type] with proper HTTP methods, status codes, and pagination. Include example request and response formats.

prompt_templates.title: Service Boundary Definition
prompt_templates.prompt: Define service boundaries for a [domain] system using Domain-Driven Design. Identify bounded contexts, aggregates, and inter-service communication patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Resilience Pattern Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Design resilience patterns for a service that depends on [external service]. Include circuit breaker configuration, retry strategy with backoff, and fallback mechanisms.

prompt_templates.title: Event-Driven Architecture Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an event-driven architecture for [business process] using [message broker]. Define event schemas, routing strategies, and handling for failure scenarios including dead letter queues.

output_examples.input: Design a REST API for an order management system
output_examples.output: Provides OpenAPI specification with endpoints for order CRUD operations, pagination using cursor-based approach, proper HTTP status codes (201 for creation, 404 for not found), and versioning strategy using URL path prefix /api/v1/

output_examples.input: How should services communicate in a microservices architecture?
output_examples.output: Recommends synchronous gRPC for low-latency internal calls, asynchronous messaging via Kafka for event propagation, and API Gateway for external client requests. Includes circuit breaker patterns and fallback strategies for each communication type.

best_practices: Design APIs contract-first with OpenAPI or GraphQL schemas before implementation
Build resilience patterns into architecture from the start rather than adding later
Keep services stateless to enable horizontal scaling and simplify deployment
anti_patterns: Shared databases between services creates tight coupling and deployment dependencies
Synchronous communication chains cause cascading failures across services
Premature microservices decomposition before understanding domain boundaries

faq.question: When should I use this skill versus database-architect?
faq.answer: Use backend-architect for service design, API contracts, and inter-service communication. Use database-architect for schema design, indexing strategies, and database-specific optimization. Backend-architect defers data layer decisions to database-architect.

faq.question: Does this skill generate implementation code?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on architectural guidance, design patterns, and technical decisions. It provides API contracts, diagrams, and recommendations but defers actual code implementation to coding-focused skills.

faq.question: What architecture styles does this skill support?
faq.answer: Supports monolithic, microservices, event-driven, serverless, and hybrid architectures. The skill helps choose the appropriate style based on your scale requirements, team size, and business constraints.

faq.question: How does this skill handle security concerns?
faq.answer: Incorporates security patterns including authentication, authorization, rate limiting, and input validation. For comprehensive security audits and penetration testing guidance, use the security-auditor skill.

faq.question: Can this skill help with cloud provider selection?
faq.answer: Provides general cloud-native patterns and deployment strategies. For specific cloud provider selection and infrastructure-as-code guidance, use the cloud-architect skill for detailed recommendations.

faq.question: What output formats does this skill provide?
faq.answer: Provides API specifications in OpenAPI or GraphQL schema format, architecture diagrams using Mermaid notation, written documentation including ADRs, and technology recommendations with rationale.
